should_trigger: Create a WBS for a multi-agent rewrite of our billing platform and make sure every deliverable has an owner and acceptance criteria.
We have a project scope statement and need to break it into work packages before building the schedule.
Audit this decomposition for missing scope, duplicated work, and whether it follows the 100% rule.
Turn these product requirements into a hierarchy of deliverables that subagents can execute and report against.
Build a WBS dictionary for this orchestration plan with element codes, boundaries, interfaces, and owners.
should_not_trigger: Make a chronological sprint plan for the next two weeks from these already-defined tickets.
Summarize the history of the Work Breakdown Structure concept.
Generate a simple personal to-do checklist for cleaning my desk.
edge_cases: We already have phases and teams listed as top-level workstreams; decide whether to keep them or rebuild the plan around deliverables.
The user asks for a WBS but only provides a vague idea. Produce a preliminary WBS with explicit assumptions instead of inventing a baseline.
A branch is a support function like project management or risk tracking. Include it only if it is necessary enabling work with clear boundaries.
